req.title: Wire transfer
req.statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three distinct decision branches: (1) valid 2FA + amount <= $100,000 → 200/queued, (2) valid 2FA + amount > $100,000 → 202/manual review, (3) stale/missing 2FA → 401. BVA is applied at the $100,000 threshold with values $99,999.99, $100,000.00, and $100,000.01. EP covers the normal-path partition (small transfer). NEG covers the stale-2FA partition. STC covers the 2FA state transition from verified→stale — this is a POSITIVE test of the transition mechanism (confirming the system correctly enforces the state boundary) and is therefore classified as kind=positive per the rule that STC heuristic must not be paired with kind=negative. DT covers the combined condition (amount band × 2FA state).

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_below_threshold_queued
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance
actions: POST /transfers/wire with valid 2FA token and amount=50000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-006: wire queued; compliance check started
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_below_threshold_queued(api_client, fresh_2fa_token, wire_payload_factory):
    payload = wire_payload_factory(amount=50000.00)
    headers = {"X-2FA-Token": fresh_2fa_token}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "queued"
    assert body.get("compliance_check") == "started"
    assert body.get("manual_review", False) is False

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401
actions: POST /transfers/wire with stale 2FA token and amount=10000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error indicating 2FA is stale or expired
trace: R-FIN-006: precondition 2fa verified in last 5 min
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401(api_client, stale_2fa_token, wire_payload_factory):
    payload = wire_payload_factory(amount=10000.00)
    headers = {"X-2FA-Token": stale_2fa_token}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_msg = body.get("error", "").lower()
    assert any(kw in error_msg for kw in ("2fa", "expired", "stale", "authentication"))

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_at_exact_threshold_queued_no_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance
actions: POST /transfers/wire with valid 2FA token and amount=100000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: manual_review is false (at-boundary does NOT trigger manual review)
trace: R-FIN-006: transfers above $100,000 require manual review (at = not above)
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_at_exact_threshold_queued_no_manual_review(api_client, fresh_2fa_token, wire_payload_factory):
    payload = wire_payload_factory(amount=100000.00)
    headers = {"X-2FA-Token": fresh_2fa_token}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "queued"
    assert body.get("manual_review", False) is False, "Exact threshold must NOT trigger manual review"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance
Transfer amount is $100,000.01 (one cent above threshold)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with valid 2FA token and amount=100000.01
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains status='pending_review' or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-006: error pathway 202 when manual review required
R-FIN-006: transfers above $100,000 require manual review
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_requires_manual_review(api_client, fresh_2fa_token, wire_payload_factory):
    payload = wire_payload_factory(amount=100000.01)
    headers = {"X-2FA-Token": fresh_2fa_token}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202 for manual review, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True, "Amount above threshold must set manual_review=true"
    assert body.get("compliance_check") == "started"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_2fa_state_transition_verified_to_stale
preconditions: Simulated system time advances 2FA age beyond 5-minute window
actions: Obtain a 2FA token timestamped exactly 5 minutes and 1 second ago via fixture
POST /transfers/wire with that token and amount=20000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 401 status code confirming system correctly enforces the verified→expired state transition
System transitions 2FA state from valid to stale/expired as designed
No transfer is created, validating state enforcement is active
trace: R-FIN-006: precondition 2fa verified in last 5 min
R-FIN-006: state transition valid→expired triggers 401 — positive confirmation that state machine enforces the boundary correctly
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_2fa_state_transition_verified_to_stale(api_client, wire_payload_factory, expired_boundary_2fa_token):
    # expired_boundary_2fa_token is timestamped 5 min + 1 sec ago
    # This positive STC test confirms the system correctly enforces the state transition
    payload = wire_payload_factory(amount=20000.00)
    headers = {"X-2FA-Token": expired_boundary_2fa_token}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401 after 2FA expiry, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "2fa" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "expired" in body.get("error", "").lower()

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_decision_table_large_amount_stale_2fa
actions: POST /transfers/wire with stale 2FA token and amount=200000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 401 status code (authentication check precedes amount check)
No transfer queued and no manual review initiated
trace: R-FIN-006: 401 when 2fa stale takes precedence over 202 manual review
R-FIN-006: decision table condition combination stale-2FA AND amount>100k
tests.executable:
def test_wire_transfer_decision_table_large_amount_stale_2fa(api_client, stale_2fa_token, wire_payload_factory):
    payload = wire_payload_factory(amount=200000.00)
    headers = {"X-2FA-Token": stale_2fa_token}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/wire", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Stale 2FA must return 401 regardless of amount, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("manual_review", False) is False, "No manual review should be initiated when 2FA is stale"
